message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Cheesecake Factory (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: 1000+ on Yelp (1,072 reviews); additional volume on TripAdvisor and Google. Aggregate exposure across platforms in the multi-thousand range.
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: [UNVERIFIED] exact opening year at The River; The River shopping center opened in 2002 and Cheesecake Factory has been a long-standing anchor tenant. Operated by The Cheesecake Factory Incorporated (publicly traded, NASDAQ: CAKE), ownership stable.
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably in: 'restaurants at The River Rancho Mirage', 'chain restaurants Palm Springs area', 'Cheesecake Factory Coachella Valley'. Does NOT surface in curated/editorial queries like 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert', 'date night Rancho Mirage' (Palm Springs Life, Locale Magazine, OpenTable editorial lists skip it in favor of Wally's Desert Turtle, Edge Steakhouse, Acqua California Bistro, Cowboy Cantina, Willie's Modern Fare). Appears in general 'casual dining' and 'family-friendly' query contexts.
- Site structured data: Brand maintains a dedicated location page at locations.thecheesecakefactory.com/ca/rancho-mirage-81.html with clean address, phone (760-404-1400), full hours (Mon-Thu 11a-10p, Fri-Sat 11a-11p, Sun 10a-10p), brunch and happy-hour callouts, and parking signals. No JSON-LD schema.org markup observable in fetched content — a gap for a corporate-grade site. Menu structure described in prose rather than structured data.
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# The Cheesecake Factory - Rancho Mirage Location

## Contact Information
**Address:** 71800 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270
**Phone:** +1 760-404-1400

## Hours of Operation
- Monday-Thursday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM
- Friday-Saturday: 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM
- Sunday: 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM

## Brunch & Happy Hour
"Brunch is available Saturday-Sunday until 2:00 PM" and "Happy Hour is served in the bar area Monday-Friday from 4:00 PM-6:00 PM" with select food and drink specials.

## Parking
The location offers "Free Self Parking" and "Valet Parking" options.

## Menu Structure
The restaurant features "more than 250 dishes, freshly prepared from scratch" daily, including appetizers, salads, burgers, sandwiches, pasta, seafood, and specialties. "More than 40 legendary cheesecakes and desserts" are available, along with a SkinnyLicious® menu option and kids' menu.

## Fine Dining Options

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine.

Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert is known for its fresh catches and extensive wine list, offering a sophisticated yet relaxed dining experience with a range of seafood options from oysters to Chilean sea bass.

Located at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, The Edge Steakhouse offers a premier dining experience with stunning views of the Coachella Valley, specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood with floor-to-ceiling windows.

Jackalope Ranch is a popular restaurant and event venue located in Indio, California, known for its rustic and lively atmosphere with a diverse menu featuring Southwestern and American cuisine.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about fine dining in Palm Desert according to Palm Springs Life and related 2026 editorials:

## Palm Desert Fine Dining Recognition

In Palm Desert and La Quinta, high-end restaurants with exceptional seafood, steak, and world-class wine lists round out the culinary map.

## Palm Springs Life's Best Fine Dining Winners

Palm Springs Life invites readers to vote for their favorite businesses, experiences, and attractions across Greater Palm Springs in their city-by-city Best of the Best competition, with winners for Best Fine Dining. Notable establishments featured in Palm Springs Life coverage include:

- **Wally's Desert Turtle** (Rancho Mirage): Founded in 1978, Wally's is old-school sophisticated dining, with an elaborate interior designed by Steve Chase and a menu of classics like escargot, Dover sole, and Wally's soufflés.

- **Eddie V's** (Palm Desert): The El Paseo restaurant buoys diners with a posh happy hour, live jazz music, and enhancements to seafood and steaks including a cave-aged blue cheese crust.

The 2025 voting results are the most recent available, with the next round of voting opening in April 2026.

I found several excellent date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage based on the search results:

## Fine Dining Options

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Located at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, this elegant steakhouse serves premium American Wagyu steaks that are dry-aged in house, along with seafood and signature cocktails. The restaurant features floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the desert landscape.

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Founded in 1978, this restaurant features a stellar menu crafted by French culinary expert Jean-Louis Jalouneix and elegant classical interior design with candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals.

**Shame on the Moon** - Operating since 1984, this fine-dining establishment offers intimate and elegant dual dining rooms as well as a beautiful moonlit patio area.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - This chic restaurant serves artfully plated dishes balancing traditional European technique with California flavors, and is a go-to for date nights with warm service and sleek modern elegance.

## Japanese Dining

**Shabu Shabu Zen** - This cozy Japanese dining experience offers premium Wagyu beef and fresh seafood, and earned a spot on Yelp's "100 Best Places to Eat" list in 2017.

## Italian Dining

**Enzo's Bistro and Bar** - Popular for date night, it features a warm, old-school Italian ambiance with a large outdoor patio with heaters, fireplace and water features, plus attentive service.

## Newer Option

**Double Date** at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa is a new dining experience by executive chef Jose Lopez that celebrates California and Italian cuisines, inspired by the Coachella Valley's reputation as the "date capital of the world."

Similarly, the document does not specify when The River shopping center itself opened. To find this information, you would need to consult additional sources, such as The River's official history or press releases.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheesecake Factory:

**Trading Information:**
The Cheesecake Factory trades on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol CAKE.

**Public Company Status:**
The Cheesecake Factory Incorporated was founded in 1972 and is headquartered in Calabasas, California. The company is publicly traded on the NASDAQ stock exchange.

**Current Stock Details:**
As of the most recent data available, Cheesecake Factory stock price per share is $60.20 (as of May 1, 2026), and the total value of Cheesecake Factory stock (market capitalization) is $3.00B.

**Business Operations:**
The company operates restaurants under the brands comprising The Cheesecake Factory, North Italia, Flower Child, and Fox Restaurant Concepts.

Brunch (Sat-Sun until 2pm) and Happy Hour (Mon-Fri 4-6pm) callouts present on brand location page

Operated by The Cheesecake Factory Incorporated, publicly traded NASDAQ: CAKE — confirmed (ticker CAKE, market cap ~$3B, HQ Calabasas)

Does NOT surface in curated/editorial date-night or fine-dining lists for Rancho Mirage / Coachella Valley — confirmed: Locale Magazine, Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, and Visit Greater Palm Springs date-night and fine-dining roundups all feature Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Shame on the Moon, Willie's Modern Fare, Enzo's, Double Date — Cheesecake Factory is absent from all of them

'Long-standing anchor tenant' status — The River's own page for the restaurant does not classify it as an anchor; no independent source surfaced confirming anchor designation
Sentiment 'mostly positive' — did not pull aggregate star rating from Yelp/Google/TripAdvisor to verify the sentiment characterization
Additional 'multi-thousand' aggregate review volume across TripAdvisor and Google — not independently verified beyond the 1,072 Yelp count
Visibility gap score of 2 and 'anchor-reference' convertibility tier — internal scoring framework, not externally verifiable but consistent with observed surfacing pattern
input.notes: Core visibility thesis holds up well: brand location page exists with clean NAP/hours but lacks JSON-LD (independently confirmed by fetch); restaurant surfaces in branded/chain queries but is entirely absent from editorial date-night and fine-dining roundups (Locale Magazine, Palm Springs Life, OpenTable, Visit Greater Palm Springs) — confirmed via search. Corporate ownership (CAKE/NASDAQ) verified. Main gaps are around longevity claims (opening year, anchor-tenant status) which remain unverified rather than refuted, and the sentiment claim which wasn't drilled into. No claims contradicted.
